q-oni-oniart2-2bf27aad-ee65754e.stem: What is a “pushed convoy” according to the ONI?
choices: An assembly of non-propelled vessels joined together into a rigid unit, pushed by at least one motorised vessel
An assembly of non-propelled vessels towed by at least one motorised vessel
Any group of pleasure craft navigating together
q-oni-oniart2-2bf27aad-ee65754e.explanation: ONI art. 2: the pushed convoy is a rigid unit that is pushed; if towed = towed convoy.

q-rnl-rnlart64-284d0c2c-7ddb9699.stem: When meeting, which vessels must every vessel — apart from priority passenger vessels and towed convoys — keep clear of?
q-rnl-rnlart64-284d0c2c-7ddb9699.explanation: RNL art. 64, para. 2 let. b: it keeps clear of cargo vessels.

q-rnl-rnlart64-284d0c2c-ac1fdc24.stem: Faced with a vessel unable to manoeuvre that signals its presence, what must every other vessel do?
choices: Overtake it on the starboard side
q-rnl-rnlart64-284d0c2c-ac1fdc24.explanation: RNL art. 64, para. 1: every vessel must keep clear of a vessel unable to manoeuvre that signals its presence.

q-oni-oniart134-df53e657-aedca456.stem: Which of these devices are INDIVIDUAL lifesaving appliances within the meaning of the ONI?
q-oni-oniart134-df53e657-aedca456.explanation: ONI art. 134: lifejackets with collars and lifebuoys = individual; boarding life rafts are collective.

q-matelotagewp-matelotagenœuddechaiseroi.stem: Why is the bowline described as the “king of knots”?
choices: It is a reliable loop knot that does not slip under tension and unties easily
It tightens more and more under tension until it becomes impossible to untie
It is used only to join two ropes of the same diameter
q-matelotagewp-matelotagenœuddechaiseroi.explanation: The bowline is a reliable loop that does not slip and unties easily.

q-meteovents-météosuisselabise-22689f11-.stem: During which period is the Bise clearly dominant on Lake Geneva?
choices: In the first half of the year, particularly in spring
In high summer, from July to August
q-meteovents-météosuisselabise-22689f11-.explanation: MeteoSwiss: the Bise is dominant in the first half of the year, especially in spring.
